rag_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL model structure during fusion
Preserve existing component types, implementations, features, connections, and properties
Do not modify the ownership of components or features during fusion
Ensure that all inserted AGREE annex blocks are syntactically correct and valid in their respective scopes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate scope based on the component type or implementation
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
anti_patterns: Do not modify the original AADL architecture during fusion
Do not place AGREE annex blocks in incorrect scopes
Do not use identifiers that are not visible in the current scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names and unit tokens in AGREE annexes
